title: hieracium pilosum subsp. faucillense
intro: Hieracium pilosum subsp. faucillense, also known as the Rough Hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family, native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The leaves and stems of the plant are used in herbal medicine, and it is known to have medicinal properties such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Hieracium pilosum subsp. faucillense include reducing inflammation and improving digestive health, making it a popular remedy for various gastrointestinal issues. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, from digestive issues to skin problems.
